STATE CAPITOL
P.O. BOX 942848
SACRAMENTO, CA
95814
ROOM 5047
(916) 445-3648
FAX (916) 327 2272

California State Senate

COMMITTEES:
APPROPRIATIONS
HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS
VICE CHAIRMAN
TOXICS AND PUBLIC
SAFETY ENFORCEMENT
VICE CHAIRMAN
INDUSTRIAL RELATIONS
SELECT COMMITTEE ON
BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT
CHAIRMAN
SELECT COMMITTEE ON
STATE PROCUREMENT AND
EXPENDITURE REVIEW
JOINT COMMITTEE ON
SCHOOL FACILITIES

ROBERT J MCKENZIE, JR
CHIEF OF STAFF

PETE WILSON, CHAIRMAN
AMERICAN LEGISLATIVE
EXCHANGE COUNCIL

SENATOR
BILL LEONARD

CHAIRMAN
SENATE REPUBLICAN CAUCUS

July 18, 1991

Jeanne Pritchard
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
482 J Street, Suite 610
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Pritchard:

I am requesting that the Commission provide me with a formal
written opinion addressing my duties and obligations under
the conflict-of-interest laws if my wife were to be employed
by a firm registered to lobby the California Legislature.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

BILL LEONARD
STATE SENATOR

BL:gr

embedding.qa_text: The letter is from Senator Bill Leonard of the California State Senate to Jeanne Pritchard, who works for the Fair Political Practices Commission in Sacramento. The senator is requesting a formal written opinion from the commission regarding his duties and obligations under conflict-of-interest laws if his wife were to be employed by a firm registered to lobby the California Legislature. The senator expresses gratitude for the commission's attention to this matter and signs off with "Sincerely," followed by his signature and title as State Senator. The letter is dated July 18, 1991, and includes the senator's contact information and the commission's address. The image you've provided is completely white, with no visible content or text. If you have another page or section of the document that you would like to discuss or transcribe, please provide it.
